expectations.text: Primary keyword 'how to use miso' appears within the first 200 words
expectations.evidence: Line 23: 'This guide covers how to use miso across a full range of dishes'

expectations.text: Body contains at least 4 Markdown internal links [text](/path)
expectations.evidence: 10 internal links per audit: miso, traditional-chinese-medicine, fermented-black-beans, fermented-tofu, ginger, reishi-mushroom, shiitake-mushrooms, tofu, soy-sauce, miso-soup-ginger-reishi
First sentence: '[Miso](/ingredients/miso) is one of the oldest fermented foods in continuous production'

expectations.text: Post includes the 'add miso off-heat' technique (don't boil miso)
expectations.evidence: Entire section 'How to Use Miso Without Killing the Bacteria' covers the off-heat technique in detail with specific instructions

expectations.text: YAML frontmatter has title, slug, tags, and meta_description
expectations.evidence: All fields present. Tags include miso, fermented, probiotic, gut-health, tcm

expectations.text: Body does not open with a # H1 heading
expectations.evidence: Body opens with prose paragraph. First heading is ## Where Miso Comes From: China, Korea, Japan

expectations.text: Post includes a FAQ section with at least 2 questions
expectations.evidence: 4 FAQ questions: 'What does miso taste like?', 'Can I use any type of miso interchangeably?', 'Does miso go bad?', 'How much miso should I use per serving?'

expectations.text: Post does not end with a boilerplate disclaimer paragraph
expectations.evidence: Ends with a CTA paragraph pointing to the miso ingredient page and miso-soup-ginger-reishi recipe. No disclaimer.

expectations.text: YAML includes an internal_links_used audit field
